1.text: We have assuredly given you, O Muhammad (s), Abundance — [the name of] a river in Paradise and his [the Prophet's] pool around which his community shall gather; al-kawthar also means the abundant good [that has been given to the Prophet], such as prophethood, the Qur'n, intercession, and the like.

2.text: So pray to your Lord, the prayer of the Festival of Immolation (`d al-nahr), and sacrifice, your offering.

3.text: Indeed it is your antagonist, your hater, who is the severed one, refraining from all [acts of] good, or the one whose line of offspring will be severed [by his not having any]: this was revealed regarding al-`s b. W'il, who called the Prophet (s) al-abtar, `the severed one', when his son al-Qsim died. Meccan or Medinese, consisting of 6 verses; this was revealed when a band of idolaters said to the Prophet (s), `What if you worship our gods for one year, and we worship your God the next?'
